result.name: Velat Doshbes

sheet.name: Velat Doshbes
sheet.sentence: Velat Doshbes is Exiled who Travels Through Time.

skills: Trained in all tasks involving foraging, hunting, and finding safe places to rest or hide
Trained in all tasks involving sneaking
inabilities: Living on your own for as long as you have makes you slow to trust others and awkward in social situations

chosen_skills.name: Trained in all tasks involving foraging, hunting, and finding safe places to rest or hide
Trained in all tasks involving sneaking
Living on your own for as long as you have makes you slow to trust others and awkward in social situations

attacks.name: Heavy Weapon
attacks.description: A heavy weapon of your choice. Granted from Starting Equipment. Requires two hands.

attacks.name: Medium Weapon
attacks.description: A medium weapon of your choice. Granted from Starting Equipment.

attacks.name: Punch
attacks.description: A right jab. Attacks are eased by 1 for Light weapons.

cyphers.name: Intuitive Insight
cyphers.description: You notice, learn, or discover something about your current situation or location. The GM provides you with relevant, useful, and reliable information. You can ask the GM to determine the information on their own, or you can ask the GM one question relevant to your immediate situation, which they must answer truthfully with a simple, non-cryptic response.

cyphers.name: Wings
cyphers.description: User can fly at their normal running speed for ten minutes per cypher level.

equipment: You have a memento from your past-an old picture, a locket with a few strands of hair inside, or a lighter given to you by someone important. You keep the object close at hand and pull it out to help you remember better times.  Granted from Exiled.
Appropriate clothing and two weapons of your choice, plus one expensive item, two moderately priced items, and up to four inexpensive items. Granted from Starting Equipment.
Armor of your choice.  Granted from Practiced In Armor.
sheet.notes:
Possible player intrusions based on your character type:
Perfect Setup
You're fighting at least three foes and each one is standing in exactly the right spot for you to use a move you trained in long ago, allowing you to attack all three as a single action. Make a separate attack roll for each foe. You remain limited by the amount of Effort you can apply on one action.

Old Friend
A comrade in arms from your past shows up unexpectedly and provides aid in whatever you're doing. They are on a mission of their own and can't stay longer than it takes to help out, chat for a while after, and perhaps share a quick meal.

Weapon Break
Your foe's weapon has a weak spot. In the course of the combat, it quickly becomes damaged and moves two steps down the object damage track.

Self-Reliant
+2 to your Might Pool.
Granted from Exiled

Possible GM intrusion from your focus:
Paradoxes are created. Others remember past events differently.

Background:
A Warrior is also known as a Warrior

Warrior
You're a good ally to have in a fight. You know how to use weapons and defend yourself. Depending on the genre and setting in question, this might mean wielding a sword and shield in the gladiatorial arena, an AK-47 and a bandolier of grenades in a savage firefight, or a blaster rifle and powered armor when exploring an alien planet. Warriors are physical, action-oriented people. They're more likely to overcome a challenge using force than by other means, and they often take the most straightforward path toward their goals.

Exiled
You have walked a long and lonely road, leaving your home and your life behind. You might have committed a heinous crime, something so awful that your people forced you out, and if you dare return, you face death. You might have been accused of a crime you didn't commit and now must pay the price for someone else's wicked deed. Your exile might be the result of a social gaffe-perhaps you shamed your family or a friend, or you embarrassed yourself in front of your peers, an authority, or someone you respect. Whatever the reason, you have left your old life behind and now strive to make a new one.

Travels Through Time
You can see through time, try to reach through it, and eventually even travel through it.

Choose how you became involved in the adventure:
- The other PCs earned your trust by helping you when you needed it. You accompany them to repay them.
- While exploring on your own, you discovered something strange. When you traveled to a settlement, the PCs were the only ones who believed you, and they have accompanied you to help you deal with the problem.
- One of the other PCs reminds you of someone you used to know.
- You have grown weary of your isolation. Joining the other PCs gives you a chance to belong.

Background Connection:
You trained in an isolated monastery. The monks think of you as a brother, but you're a stranger to all others.

Focus Connection:
Pick one other PC. You are always trying to impress that character with your skill, wit, appearance, or bravado. Perhaps they are a rival, perhaps you need their respect, or perhaps you're romantically interested in them.

Loner: You gain no benefit when you get help with a task from another character who is trained or specialized in that task.
Granted from Exiled
Trained in all tasks involving foraging, hunting, and finding safe places to rest or hide: Granted from Exiled
Trained in all tasks involving sneaking: Granted from Exiled
Heavy weapons: Heavy Weapons
Granted from Warrior
Light weapons: Light Weapons
Granted from Warrior
Medium weapons: Medium Weapons
Granted from Warrior
Living on your own for as long as you have makes you slow to trust others and awkward in social situations: Any task involving social interaction is hindered.
Granted from Exiled
Anticipation: You look ahead to see how your actions might unfold. The first task you perform before the end of the next round gains an asset. Action.
Granted from Travels Through Time
Combat Prowess: You add +1 damage to one type of weapon attack of your choice: melee weapon attacks or ranged weapon attacks. Enabler.
Granted from Warrior
Control The Field: This melee attack inflicts 1 less point of damage than normal, and regardless of whether you hit the target, you maneuver it into a position you desire within immediate range. Action.
Granted from Warrior
Practiced In Armor: You can wear armor for long periods of time without tiring and can compensate for slowed reactions from wearing armor. You reduce the Speed cost for wearing armor by 1. You start the game with a type of armor of your choice. Enabler.
Granted from Warrior
Quick Throw: After using a thrown light weapon, you draw another light weapon and make another thrown attack against the same target or a different one. Action.
Granted from Warrior
Heavy Weapon: A heavy weapon of your choice. Granted from Starting Equipment. Requires two hands.
Medium Weapon: A medium weapon of your choice. Granted from Starting Equipment.
Punch: A right jab. Attacks are eased by 1 for Light weapons.
